title: Vue: Make globals reactive in decorators
body:
This reverts commit 07f07ec33f1ff3cbebb5d2354f553e22f1780bb4.


## Checklist for Contributors

### Testing
#### Manual testing
See https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/pull/33562

### 🦋 Canary release

<!-- CANARY_RELEASE_SECTION -->

This PR does not have a canary release associated. You can request a canary release of this pull request by mentioning the `@storybookjs/core` team here.

_core team members can create a canary release [here](https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/actions/workflows/publish.yml) or locally with `gh workflow run --repo storybookjs/storybook publish.yml --field pr=<PR_NUMBER>`_

<!-- BENCHMARK_SECTION -->
<!-- BENCHMARK_SECTION -->


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Reactive global state is now supported in Vue 3 decorators.
  * Decorators can update story args dynamically via updateArgs; new example stories demonstrate counter and locale-driven behavior.

* **Tests**
  * Added end-to-end tests covering decorator-driven arg updates and reactive globals in Vue 3.
  * Expanded unit tests to validate reactive global handling and updateArgs behavior.

* **Documentation**
  * New Vue 3 docs and snippets showing reactive globals and updateArgs usage in decorators.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
author: Sidnioulz

comments.author: coderabbitai
Sidnioulz
comments.body: @huang-julien @chakAs3 any thoughts on that? It's unrelated to the PR, but is the bunny correct here? Do we have a memleak or potential arg corruption going on?

comments.author: huang-julien
comments.body:
Oops, a bit late to the party. 
I think rabbit is right. We should probably clean up properties from old reactive object if the next one is empty. 
It's not really about memleak, but more about the reactive global not reflecting the state of storycontext.globals

comments.author: Sidnioulz
comments.body: Thanks @huang-julien! I've created https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook/issues/34319 as a follow up for contributors.

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_
🧩 Analysis chain 🌐 Web query: `In Vue 3 templates, is `:lang={{globals?.locale || 'en'}}` valid syntax, or should the binding be written as `:lang="globals?.locale || 'en'"`?` 💡 Result: ` :lang={{globals?.locale || 'en'}} ` is **not** valid Vue 3 template syntax—mustache interpolation (`{{ }}`) can’t be used inside HTML attributes. Use a `v-bind` / `:` binding with a JavaScript expression instead: ```vue :lang="globals?.locale || 'en'" ``` This is exactly what Vue’s template syntax docs describe for attribute bindings. [1] **Sources** [1] Vue.js Guide — Template Syntax (Attribute Bindings): ([vuejs.org](https://vuejs.org/guide/essentials/template-syntax.html?source=post_page-----f1acd4696b71----------------------)) Citations: - 1: https://vuejs.org/guide/essentials/template-syntax.html?source=post_page-----f1acd4696b71---------------------- ---
**Fix the `:lang` binding syntax in both Vue snippets.** `:lang={{globals?.locale || 'en'}}` is not valid Vue template syntax—mustache interpolation (`{{ }}`) cannot be used inside HTML attributes. Use a `v-bind` binding with a JavaScript expression instead: `:lang="globals?.locale || 'en'"`.
💡 Proposed fix ```diff template: ` -
+

Greeting: {{greeting}}

`, ```
Also applies to lines 40-45.
📝 Committable suggestion > ‼️ **IMPORTANT** > Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements. ```suggestion template: `

Greeting: {{greeting}}

`, ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@docs/_snippets/decorator-with-reactive-globals.md` around lines 14 - 19, The Vue template uses mustache interpolation inside an attribute (the lang attribute in the template string) which is invalid; update both template snippets to use a v-bind on the lang attribute so it receives a JavaScript expression that uses globals?.locale with a fallback of 'en' (i.e., bind the lang attribute rather than using {{ }}); locate the template blocks containing the lang attribute and replace the attribute usage accordingly in both places. ```

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`In Storybook Vue 3, should `.storybook/preview.ts` export a default `Preview` object, and would a file that only contains `const WithIncrementDecorator = { args, decorators }` be treated as valid preview configuration?`

💡 Result:

Yes—**`.storybook/preview.ts` should export Storybook “project annotations”**, and the currently recommended/typed pattern is a **default export** of a `Preview` object (e.g. `const preview: Preview = { decorators, parameters, args, ... }; export default preview;`). [1]

Storybook can also read **specific named exports** (e.g. `export const decorators = [...]`) from `preview.ts`, which it turns into internal configuration. [2]

A file that *only* contains:

```ts
const WithIncrementDecorator = { args, decorators };
```

is **not valid preview configuration by itself**, because **nothing is exported** for Storybook to consume. To make it effective, you’d need to export it (either as the `default` preview object or by exporting the relevant named fields). [1][2][3]

**Sources:**  
[1] Storybook docs – Setup / `.storybook/preview.ts` default `Preview` export pattern ([storybook.js.org](https://storybook.js.org/docs/8.6/get-started/setup))  
[2] Storybook docs – Builder API (preview config via named exports like `decorators`) ([storybook.js.org](https://storybook.js.org/docs/8/builders/builder-api?utm_source=openai))  
[3] Storybook docs – Global args configured via default export in `preview.js|ts` ([storybook.js.org](https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/args))

Citations:

- 1: https://storybook.js.org/docs/8.6/get-started/setup
- 2: https://storybook.js.org/docs/8/builders/builder-api?utm_source=openai
- 3: https://storybook.js.org/docs/writing-stories/args

---



</details>

**Snippet filename does not match the code pattern—no exports.**

The code defines `WithIncrementDecorator` as a constant but exports nothing. Storybook's `.storybook/preview.ts` requires either a default export of a `Preview` object or named exports (e.g., `export const decorators = [...]`). Pasting this code into a preview file will not register the decorator. Either show this as a `*.stories.*` example with an exported story, or convert it to a proper preview default export.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@docs/_snippets/decorator-with-updateArgs.md` around lines 1 - 28, The snippet
defines WithIncrementDecorator but never exports it, so Storybook won't register
the decorator; update the snippet to export the decorator properly by converting
the constant into a preview export (for example create a default export matching
Storybook's Preview shape that includes decorators or export a named
"decorators" array referencing the decorator), ensure the decorator uses
useArgs()/updateArgs as shown (keep the function body and template) and remove
the unused WithIncrementDecorator constant or replace it with an exported value
so Storybook's preview file receives either a default export or named export
(e.g., export const decorators = [...]) that includes the decorator.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:grasshopper -->

comments.author: Sidnioulz
comments.body: @coderabbitai Learn: files in docs/_snippets are shown in our doc site to teach users, so they aren't always functionally complete. Look at them from a doc/teaching perspective and don't mention missing imports/exports/code when it would be obvious to users how to complete the snippet.

comments.author: coderabbitai
comments.body:
`@Sidnioulz`, understood! I'll keep that in mind for future reviews — documentation snippets in `docs/_snippets` are meant to teach concepts and don't need to be functionally complete. I'll avoid flagging missing imports, exports, or boilerplate when it would be self-evident to the reader.

---

<details>
<summary>✏️ Learnings added</summary>

```
Learnt from: Sidnioulz
Repo: storybookjs/storybook PR: 34116
File: docs/_snippets/decorator-with-updateArgs.md:1-28
Timestamp: 2026-03-16T19:37:22.815Z
Learning: In the storybookjs/storybook repository, files under `docs/_snippets/` are documentation snippets displayed on the Storybook doc site for teaching/illustration purposes. They are intentionally incomplete and do not need to be functionally self-contained. Do not flag missing imports, exports, or other boilerplate code in these files when it would be obvious to users how to complete the snippet in their own project.
```

> Note: Learnings are effective only in the context of similar code segments. To apply general coding guidelines, please configure [review instructions](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/guides/review-instructions). You can manage existing learnings in the [app](https://app.coderabbit.ai/learnings).

</details>



---

<details>
<summary>🧠 Learnings used</summary>

```
Learnt from: kasperpeulen
Repo: storybookjs/storybook PR: 0
File: :0-0
Timestamp: 2026-01-28T09:16:24.155Z
Learning: In Storybook CSF factories codemod, preview files with only named exports (including re-exports like `export { withTheme } from './decorators'`) still require a default export to be added via `export default definePreview({})`, because story files import the preview default with `import preview from '../.storybook/preview'`. Named exports do not count as default exports, so the codemod correctly adds a default export even when re-exports are present.
```

---

```
Learnt from: ndelangen
Repo: storybookjs/storybook PR: 32507
File: code/core/src/manager/globals/globals-module-info.ts:25-33
Timestamp: 2025-09-24T09:39:39.233Z
Learning: In Storybook, storybook/actions/decorator is a preview-only entrypoint and should not be included in manager globals configuration. The duplicatedKeys array in code/core/src/manager/globals/globals-module-info.ts is specifically for manager-side externalization, not preview entrypoints.
```

---

Prefer WithTooltipNew/Popover; avoid suggesting changes to WithTooltip.* going forward.
```

```

```
Learning: Repo: storybookjs/storybook — Guidance: Until Storybook 11 is released, do not suggest using React.useId anywhere (e.g., in code/core/src/components/components/Select/Select.tsx) to maintain compatibility with React 17 runtimes. Prefer advising: accept a caller-provided props.id and, if needed, generate a client-only fallback id to minimize SSR hydration issues — but avoid useId. Resume prompting for useId after Storybook 11.
```
